content.text: how would you suggest i test the auth flow on prod even though i don't have another email to test with?

payload.message: how would you suggest i test the auth flow on prod even though i don't have another email to test with?

Use email aliases. Most providers support this well enough for auth testing:

```text
adamblumoff+scope-test1@gmail.com
adamblumoff+scope-test2@gmail.com
adamblumoff+scope-owner@gmail.com
adamblumoff+scope-collab@gmail.com
```

Those all deliver to `adamblumoff@gmail.com`, but most auth systems, including Clerk-backed apps, treat them as distinct users unless you normalize them yourself.

Suggested prod auth test matrix:

1. Sign up fresh with `adamblumoff+scope-owner@gmail.com`
2. Sign out
3. Sign up fresh with `adamblumoff+scope-collab@gmail.com`
4. Verify both accounts get distinct Scope users/handles
5. Test invite/collaboration/access-control flows between them
6. Test denied access by pasting a private repo/review URL from one account into the other
7. Test CLI auth using one alias, then revoke/sign out and auth as the other

For browser testing, use separate Chrome profiles or one normal window plus one incognito window so sessions don’t collide.

One caveat: if you use Google OAuth instead of email/password or magic links, plus aliases may collapse back to the same Google account depending on the provider flow. For testing distinct production users, email-code/passwordless with plus aliases is usually the cleanest path.

content.text: what do i do here?

payload.message: what do i do here?

You’re missing the `+` in the alias.

Use:

```text
adamblumoff+scope-member1@gmail.com
```

not:

```text
adamblumoff-scope-member1@gmail.com
```

Gmail plus addressing keeps delivery to your real inbox while giving Clerk a distinct email identity. Then click **Continue**.

Also, since this screen says **Sign in** and the footer says **Sign up**, if this is a brand-new alias you may need to click **Sign up** first.

tools.name: preview_status
tools.description: Report whether a collaborative browser tab is automation-capable, including its URL, title, visibility, loading state, viewport mode, and measured CSS-pixel size. Pass tabId to inspect a specific tab; omit it to use this agent session's current tab.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

tools.name: preview_navigate
tools.description: Navigate a collaborative browser tab. Pass tabId to target a specific tab, plus {url:'https://t3.chat'} for a website or {target:{kind:'environment-port',port:5173}} for a dev server. Exactly one of url or target is required.

readiness.description: Readiness milestone before returning. 'load' is the default; use 'none' only when a later wait call will verify the page.

anyOf.description: Readiness milestone before returning. 'load' waits for loading to stop (default), 'domContentLoaded' waits for an interactive document, and 'none' returns immediately.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

target.description: Environment-relative target. Prefer {kind:'environment-port',port:5173} for a dev server in the current environment.

anyOf.description: Environment-relative target. Prefer {kind:'environment-port',port:5173} for a dev server in the current environment.

kind.description: Selects a dev-server port relative to the current execution environment.

anyOf.description: Optional path, query, and fragment, for example /settings?tab=account.

allOf.description: Dev-server TCP port inside the current environment.

timeoutMs.description: Maximum wait in milliseconds. Defaults to 15000; maximum 60000.

allOf.description: Maximum wait in milliseconds. Defaults to 15000; maximum 60000.

url.description: Website URL, for example https://t3.chat. Use this for public pages and directly reachable URLs.

tools.name: preview_recording_stop
tools.description: Stop the active browser recording and save it as a local evidence artifact.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

tools.name: preview_recording_start
tools.description: Start recording the collaborative browser tab selected by tabId, or this agent session's current tab when omitted.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

tools.name: preview_resize
tools.description: Resize a collaborative browser tab, optionally selected by tabId. Use {mode:'fill'}, {mode:'freeform',width:1024,height:768}, or {mode:'preset',preset:'iphone-12-pro',orientation:'portrait'}. This changes CSS layout breakpoints without changing the desktop browser user agent.

height.description: Freeform viewport height in CSS pixels. Required only in freeform mode.

allOf.description: Freeform viewport height in CSS pixels. Required only in freeform mode.

mode.description: Viewport mode: fill follows the preview panel, freeform uses exact independently resizable dimensions, and preset uses a named device size.

orientation.description: Orientation for a named device preset. It is not accepted in fill or freeform mode.

anyOf.description: Orientation for a fixed device preset. Defaults to the preset's native orientation.

preset.description: Named device size. Required only when mode is preset.

anyOf.description: Named viewport from Chrome DevTools' standard device catalog.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

timeoutMs.description: Maximum wait in milliseconds. Defaults to 15000; maximum 60000.

allOf.description: Maximum wait in milliseconds. Defaults to 15000; maximum 60000.

width.description: Freeform viewport width in CSS pixels. Required only in freeform mode.

allOf.description: Freeform viewport width in CSS pixels. Required only in freeform mode.

tools.name: preview_open
tools.description: Show and initialize a collaborative browser tab. Pass tabId to reuse a specific existing tab, set reuseExistingTab=false to create another tab, or omit both to use this agent session's current tab.

anyOf.description: Reuse tabId when supplied, otherwise this agent session's current tab. Defaults to true; set false to create a new tab.
Whether to reveal the preview panel to the human. Defaults to true.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

url.description: Optional initial page URL, for example https://t3.chat or localhost:5173. Omit to open a blank tab.

tools.name: preview_wait_for
tools.description: Wait in the tab selected by tabId, or this agent session's current tab when omitted, until all supplied locator, selector, text, and URL conditions match.

locator.description: Playwright selector that must match an element, for example role=button[name='Send'].

selector.description: Legacy CSS selector that must match an element. Prefer locator.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

text.description: Case-sensitive substring that must appear in visible document text.

timeoutMs.description: Maximum wait in milliseconds. Defaults to 15000; maximum 60000.

allOf.description: Maximum wait in milliseconds. Defaults to 15000; maximum 60000.

urlIncludes.description: Substring that must appear in the current absolute URL.

tools.name: preview_press
tools.description: Press one keyboard key in the tab selected by tabId, or this agent session's current tab when omitted. Examples: {key:'Enter'}, {key:'Escape'}, or {key:'a',modifiers:['Meta']}.

parameters.description: Presses one keyboard key in the active browser tab.

anyOf.description: Modifier keys held while pressing key.

tabId.description: Exact collaborative browser tab to target. Omit to use this agent session's current tab.

tools.name: preview_evaluate
tools.description: Evaluate JavaScript in the tab selected by tabId, or this agent session's current tab when omitted. Returns a serializable result up to 64 KB; the expression may mutate page state.

parameters.description: Evaluates JavaScript in the page. Prefer snapshot and semantic actions; use evaluate for inspection or unsupported interactions.

anyOf.description: Await a returned Promise. Defaults to true.
Serialize and return the value instead of a remote object reference. Defaults to true.

# Browser

Use this skill for browser automation tasks such as inspecting pages, navigating, testing local apps, clicking, typing, taking screenshots, and reading visible page state.

If this plugin is listed as available in the session, treat that as mandatory reading before browser work. Open and follow this skill before saying that Browser is unavailable and before falling back to standalone Playwright or Computer Use.

Do not skip this skill just because Computer Use MCP tool calls are directly visible or appear easier to invoke. The presence of Computer Use tools is not evidence that Computer Use is the preferred browser surface.

## Setup Documentation

Use `await agent.documentation.get("bootstrap-troubleshooting")` when browser setup succeeds but discovery or selection fails.

## Bootstrap

These setup details are internal. User-facing progress updates should be less technical in nature. Never mention `Node REPL`, `node_repl`, `REPL`, JavaScript sessions, module exports, reading documentation, or loading instructions unless a user is asking for that exact information. If setup or recovery is needed, describe it naturally as connecting to the browser or retrying the browser connection.

The `browser-client` module is the core entry point for browser use, and is available under `scripts/browser-client.mjs` in this plugin's root directory. ALWAYS import it using an absolute path. IMPORTANT: If this path cannot be found, stop and report that this plugin is missing `scripts/browser-client.mjs`. NEVER use the built in `browser-client` library.

Run browser setup code through the Node REPL `js` tool. In this environment the callable tool id typically appears as `mcp__node_repl__js`. If it is not already available, use tool discovery for `node_repl js` without setting a result limit. You need the `js` execution tool: `js_reset` only clears state, and `js_add_node_module_dir` only changes package resolution. Do not call either helper while trying to expose `js`. If `js` is still not available, search again for `node_repl js` with `limit: 10`.

Initialize the runtime once per fresh Node session, select the in-app browser, and immediately read its complete documentation:

```js
if (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {
  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import("<plugin root>/scripts/browser-client.mjs");
  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });
}
globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.get("iab");
nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());
```

If setup succeeds but browser discovery or selection fails, read `await agent.documentation.get("bootstrap-troubleshooting")` before resetting the JavaScript session or trying another browser-control mechanism.

Use the browser bound to `browser` for tasks in this skill. Do not switch to another browser-control mechanism to work around authentication. If sign-in blocks requested navigation and the browser documentation does not provide a supported authentication flow, ask the user to sign in in the in-app browser and tell you when it is ready.

The ability to interact directly with the browser is exposed through the `browser-client` runtime via the `agent.browsers.*` API. Before trying to interact with it, you MUST emit and read the complete documentation returned by `await browser.documentation()` in one go. For the initial documentation read, run the exact direct call `nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());` shown above. Do not assign the documentation to a variable, inspect its length, slice it, truncate it, summarize it, or emit only an excerpt. Do not proactively split the documentation into pages or chunks. Only if the tool output itself explicitly reports that it was truncated may you emit and read smaller chunks until you have read the documentation in its entirety.

Only the Node REPL `js` tool (`mcp__node_repl__js`) can be used to control the in-app browser. Do not use external MCP browser-control tools, separate browser automation servers, or other browser skills for this surface. References to Playwright mean the in-skill `tab.playwright` API after browser-client setup.

payload.message: My first route file read hit shell expansion on the `$owner` filenames; I’m rerunning those with quoted paths. The root document has no error/pending component, so if a route throws in the wrong phase it can plausibly leave just the black page.

content.text: My first route file read hit shell expansion on the `$owner` filenames; I’m rerunning those with quoted paths. The root document has no error/pending component, so if a route throws in the wrong phase it can plausibly leave just the black page.

payload.message: I found a likely root cause in `web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.tsx`: the parent repo route’s loader returns only “live” state when it is loaded for a child route. If you then navigate back to the exact repo root, TanStack can keep the parent route loader data because the parent match/params did not change, so the component has no detail data and intentionally returns `null`. That matches a fully blank page that a refresh fixes. I’m checking the generated route tree and live-refresh invalidation now to make sure there isn’t a second contributor.

content.text: I found a likely root cause in `web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.tsx`: the parent repo route’s loader returns only “live” state when it is loaded for a child route. If you then navigate back to the exact repo root, TanStack can keep the parent route loader data because the parent match/params did not change, so the component has no detail data and intentionally returns `null`. That matches a fully blank page that a refresh fixes. I’m checking the generated route tree and live-refresh invalidation now to make sure there isn’t a second contributor.

payload.message:
I found the cause. It’s not auth; it’s the repo route loader caching the wrong shape of data.

In [repos.$owner.$repo.tsx](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.tsx:55), the parent repo route does this:

- If URL is exactly `/repos/:owner/:repo`, load full repo `detail`.
- If URL is a child path like `/settings`, `/review`, or `/history`, load only lightweight `live` state.
- Later, when rendering the repo root, if the cached parent data is still `kind: 'live'`, it returns `null` at [line 104](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.tsx:104).

That explains the blank page exactly.

Why it feels intermittent: it depends how you reached the route. If you first loaded the repo root, the parent route has `detail` cached and going around works. If you entered through `/settings`, `/review`, `/history`, or got redirected from review back to the repo root, the parent route may still have only `live` cached. Then `/repos/adamblumoff/scope-vcs` renders nothing until refresh. Refresh fixes it because the loader runs fresh at the exact repo-root URL and gets full `detail`.

The fix I’d make is to stop making the shared parent route loader depend on `location.pathname`. Split the route responsibilities so the parent/layout owns shared live state, and the repo overview has its own loader that always loads `detail`. At minimum, remove the `return null` dead-end and force a detail reload when root is displayed with `kind: 'live'`, but the cleaner fix is a route split.

content.text:
I found the cause. It’s not auth; it’s the repo route loader caching the wrong shape of data.

In [repos.$owner.$repo.tsx](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.tsx:55), the parent repo route does this:

- If URL is exactly `/repos/:owner/:repo`, load full repo `detail`.
- If URL is a child path like `/settings`, `/review`, or `/history`, load only lightweight `live` state.
- Later, when rendering the repo root, if the cached parent data is still `kind: 'live'`, it returns `null` at [line 104](/home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.tsx:104).

That explains the blank page exactly.

At minimum, remove the `return null` dead-end and force a detail reload when root is displayed with `kind: 'live'`, but the cleaner fix is a route split.

payload.message:
I’d fix it by removing the parent route’s dependency on the current pathname.

Concrete plan:

1. Change `web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.tsx` so it is just the repo layout/shared parent.
   - Its loader will always load only `live` state.
   - Its component will always render `<Outlet />`.
   - It will keep `useRepoLiveRefresh(live, invalidate)` because that is shared across repo subroutes.
   - It will stop returning `null`.

2. Move the actual repo overview page into its own index child route.
   - Add a route file for the exact repo page, likely `web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.index.tsx` if TanStack’s generator accepts that naming.
   - That index route loader will always call `loadRepo`.
   - If `detail.review` exists, it will redirect to `/repos/$owner/$repo/review`.
   - Its component will render `RepoDetailPage`.

3. Leave the child routes as children of the same parent:
   - `repos.$owner.$repo.settings.tsx`
   - `repos.$owner.$repo.review.tsx`
   - `repos.$owner.$repo.history.tsx`

4. Regenerate/let Vite regenerate `web/src/routeTree.gen.ts`.
   - I’ll verify the generated tree has `/repos/$owner/$repo/` or the route index wired as the default child under `/repos/$owner/$repo`.

5. Add a regression test where possible.
   - At minimum, test the route/data logic by ensuring the repo overview route can’t render from `kind: 'live'`.
   - If route-level testing is awkward in this setup, I’ll keep the code shape simple enough that the bug class is removed rather than patched.

6. Verify manually with the local dev server:
   - Start from `/repos/:owner/:repo/settings`, click/back-navigate to `/repos/:owner/:repo`.
   - Start from `/repos/:owner/:repo/review`, trigger the redirect/no-review path if possible.
   - Start from `/repos/:owner/:repo/history`, navigate back to repo root.
   - Confirm no blank page and no console route errors.

The key architectural change is: parent route owns shared repo context only; repo overview owns repo detail. That removes the cached “live-only parent data displayed as repo root” state entirely.

+import {
+  loadRepoForRequest,
+  parseRepoParams,
+  parseSetRepoFileVisibilityInput,
+  setRepoFileVisibilityForRequest,
+} from '@/api/repos'
+import type { RepoParams, ReviewFile, Visibility } from '@/api/types'
+import { RepoDetailPage } from '@/features/repo-detail/repo-detail-page'
+import { createFileRoute, redirect } from '@tanstack/react-router'
+import { createServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'
+
+const loadRepo = createServerFn({ method: 'GET' })
+  .validator(parseRepoParams)
+  .handler(({ data }) => loadRepoForRequest(data))
+
+const setRepoFileVisibility = createServerFn({ method: 'POST' })
+  .validator(parseSetRepoFileVisibilityInput)
+  .handler(({ data }) => setRepoFileVisibilityForRequest(data))
+
+export const Route = createFileRoute('/repos/$owner/$repo/')({
+  loader: async ({ params }) => {
+    const detail = await loadRepo({ data: params })
+    if (detail.review) {
+      throw redirect({
+        params,
+        to: '/repos/$owner/$repo/review',
+      })
+    }
+    return detail
+  },
+  component: RepoIndexRoute,
+})
+
+function RepoIndexRoute() {
+  const detail = Route.useLoaderData()
+  const params = Route.useParams()
+
+  return (
+    <RepoDetailPage
+      detail={detail}
+      setFileVisibility={setLiveRepoFileVisibility}
+      params={params}
+    />
+  )
+}
+
+function setLiveRepoFileVisibility(
+  params: RepoParams,
+  files: ReviewFile[],
+  visibility: Visibility,
+) {
+  return setRepoFileVisibility({
+    data: {
+      ...params,
+      paths: files.map((file) => file.path),
+      visibility,
+    },
+  })
+}

<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.


## T3 Code collaborative browser

You are running inside T3 Code. The `t3-code` MCP server is the product-native collaborative browser shared with the user. When it exposes `preview_*` tools, prefer those tools for browser navigation, inspection, interaction, screenshots, and recordings.

For browser work, first call `preview_status`. If no automation-capable preview is attached, call `preview_open` before concluding that the browser is unavailable. Then use `preview_navigate`, `preview_snapshot`, and the focused interaction tools. Prefer snapshot-provided locators over coordinates.

Do not switch to global browser skills, Chrome, Node REPL browser automation, standalone Playwright, or agent-browser merely because the preview is initially closed or a first call fails. Use an alternative browser system only when the T3 preview tools are absent, the user explicitly requests another browser, or `preview_open` returns an explicit unsupported/unavailable error. A failed T3 preview tool call should be inspected and retried with corrected arguments when the error is actionable.

</collaboration_mode>

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

COMMAND="${1:-status}"
case "$COMMAND" in
  doctor|up|down|status|reset) ;;
  *)
    echo "usage: $0 [doctor|up|down|status|reset]" >&2
    exit 2
    ;;
esac

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
STATE_DIR="$ROOT/.tmp/local-dev"
DATA_DIR="$ROOT/.scope/dev"
OBJECT_DIR="$DATA_DIR/objects"
KEY_FILE="$DATA_DIR/object-key.txt"
API_PID_FILE="$STATE_DIR/api.pid"
WEB_PID_FILE="$STATE_DIR/web.pid"
API_OUT_LOG="$STATE_DIR/api.out.log"
API_ERR_LOG="$STATE_DIR/api.err.log"
WEB_OUT_LOG="$STATE_DIR/web.out.log"
WEB_ERR_LOG="$STATE_DIR/web.err.log"

if [[ -d "$HOME/.local/share/scope-vcs-tools/node-24/bin" ]]; then
  export PATH="$HOME/.local/share/scope-vcs-tools/node-24/bin:$PATH"
fi
if [[ -d "$HOME/.cargo/bin" ]]; then
  export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
fi

ensure_dir() {
  mkdir -p "$1"
}

read_dotenv_value() {
  local path="$1"
  local key="$2"
  [[ -f "$path" ]] || return 0

  awk -v key="$key" '
    /^[[:space:]]*#/ { next }
    /^[[:space:]]*$/ { next }
    {
      split($0, parts, "=")
      name = parts[1]
      gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", name)
      if (name != key) {
        next
      }
      value = substr($0, index($0, "=") + 1)
      gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", value)
      if ((substr(value, 1, 1) == "\"" && substr(value, length(value), 1) == "\"") ||
          (substr(value, 1, 1) == "'"'"'" && substr(value, length(value), 1) == "'"'"'")) {
        value = substr(value, 2, length(value) - 2)
      }
      print value
      exit
    }
  ' "$path"
}

get_clerk_issuer() {
  local publishable_key payload mod clerk_host
  publishable_key="$(read_dotenv_value "$ROOT/web/.env.local" "VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY")"
  if [[ -z "$publishable_key" ]]; then
    echo "web/.env.local must define VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  if [[ "$publishable_key" != pk_test_* ]]; then
    echo "local dev requires a Clerk development publishable key (pk_test_)" >&2
    exit 1
  fi

  payload="${publishable_key#pk_test_}"
  payload="${payload//_/\/}"
  payload="${payload//-/+}"
  mod=$(( ${#payload} % 4 ))
  if [[ "$mod" -eq 2 ]]; then
    payload="${payload}=="
  elif [[ "$mod" -eq 3 ]]; then
    payload="${payload}="
  fi

  if ! clerk_host="$(printf '%s' "$payload" | base64 --decode 2>/dev/null)"; then
    echo "VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY is not valid base64url" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  clerk_host="${clerk_host%$}"
  if [[ "$clerk_host" != *.clerk.accounts.dev ]]; then
    echo "local dev Clerk issuer must end with .clerk.accounts.dev, got $clerk_host" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  printf 'https://%s\n' "$clerk_host"
}

get_dev_user_email() {
  local email
  email="$(read_dotenv_value "$ROOT/.env.local" "SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL")"
  if [[ -z "$email" ]]; then
    echo "root .env.local must define SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL for seeded local repos" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
  printf '%s\n' "$email"
}

get_dev_user_handle() {
  read_dotenv_value "$ROOT/.env.local" "SCOPE_DEV_USER_HANDLE"
}

get_object_key() {
  ensure_dir "$DATA_DIR"
  if [[ ! -f "$KEY_FILE" ]]; then
    openssl rand -base64 32 > "$KEY_FILE"
  fi
  tr -d '\r\n' < "$KEY_FILE"
}

assert_command() {
  if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "$1 is required for local dev" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

port_owner() {
  local port="$1"
  ss -H -ltnp "sport = :$port" 2>/dev/null |
    sed -n 's/.*pid=\([0-9][0-9]*\).*/\1/p' |
    head -n 1
}

assert_port_free() {
  local port="$1"
  local name="$2"
  local owner
  owner="$(port_owner "$port")"
  if [[ -n "$owner" ]]; then
    echo "$name port $port is already in use by PID $owner" >&2
    exit 1
  fi
}

stop_pid_file() {
  local pid_file="$1"
  local pid
  [[ -f "$pid_file" ]] || return 0
  pid="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < "$pid_file")"
  if [[ "$pid" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
    kill -TERM -- "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    for _ in {1..30}; do
      kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || break
      sleep 0.1
    done
    if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
      kill -KILL -- "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
  fi
  rm -f "$pid_file"
}

stop_owned_processes() {
  stop_pid_file "$API_PID_FILE"
  stop_pid_file "$WEB_PID_FILE"
}

unset DATABASE_URL
export SCOPE_ENV="local"
export SCOPE_METADATA_STORE="memory"
export SCOPE_OBJECT_STORE="filesystem"
export SCOPE_DATA_DIR="$DATA_DIR"
export SCOPE_OBJECT_STORE_DIR="$OBJECT_DIR"
export SCOPE_OBJECT_ENCRYPTION_KEY="$object_key"

export SCOPE_APP_ORIGIN="http://localhost:3000"

export CLERK_JWKS_URL="$issuer/.well-known/jwks.json"
export CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES="http://localhost:3000"
export SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL="$dev_email"
EOF
  if [[ -n "$dev_handle" ]]; then

start_web() {

fn configured_list_contains(values: &str, expected: &str) -> bool {
    values
        .split(',')
        .map(str::trim)
        .any(|value| value == expected)
}

fn dev_seed_user(snapshot: &DevEnvSnapshot) -> anyhow::Result<DevSeedUser> {
    let email = snapshot
        .dev_user_email
        .as_deref()
        .map(normalize_email)
        audiences
    }
}

fn configured_list(name: &str) -> Vec<String> {
    non_empty_env(name)
        .into_iter()
        .flat_map(|value| {
            value
                .split(',')
                .map(normalize_claim_value)
                .filter(|value| !value.is_empty())
                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        })
        .collect()
}

fn normalize_claim_value(value: &str) -> String {
    value.trim().trim_end_matches('/').to_string()

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs/dev/scope-dev
@@
 API_OUT_LOG="$STATE_DIR/api.out.log"
 API_ERR_LOG="$STATE_DIR/api.err.log"
 WEB_OUT_LOG="$STATE_DIR/web.out.log"
 WEB_ERR_LOG="$STATE_DIR/web.err.log"
+LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN="http://localhost:3000"
+LOCAL_API_ORIGIN="http://localhost:8080"
+PUBLIC_HOST="${SCOPE_DEV_PUBLIC_HOST:-}"
+
+if [[ -z "$PUBLIC_HOST" ]] && command -v tailscale >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  PUBLIC_HOST="$(tailscale ip -4 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)"
+fi
+PUBLIC_HOST="${PUBLIC_HOST:-localhost}"
+PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN="http://$PUBLIC_HOST:3000"
+PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN="http://$PUBLIC_HOST:8080"
+CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE="$LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN"
+if [[ "$PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN" != "$LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN" ]]; then
+  CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE="$CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE,$PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN"
+fi
 
 if [[ -d "$HOME/.local/share/scope-vcs-tools/node-24/bin" ]]; then
   export PATH="$HOME/.local/share/scope-vcs-tools/node-24/bin:$PATH"
 fi
@@
 export SCOPE_OBJECT_STORE_DIR="$OBJECT_DIR"
 export SCOPE_OBJECT_ENCRYPTION_KEY="$object_key"
 export PORT="8080"
-export SCOPE_APP_ORIGIN="http://localhost:3000"
-export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="http://localhost:8080"
+export SCOPE_APP_ORIGIN="$LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN"
+export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="$LOCAL_API_ORIGIN"
 export CLERK_ISSUER="$issuer"
 export CLERK_JWKS_URL="$issuer/.well-known/jwks.json"
-export CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES="http://localhost:3000"
+export CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES="$CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE"
 export SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL="$dev_email"
 EOF
@@
 done < <(env)
 export SCOPE_API_INTERNAL_URL="http://localhost:8080"
-export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="http://localhost:8080"
-exec pnpm dev
+export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="$PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN"
+exec pnpm dev --host 0.0.0.0
 EOF
   chmod +x "$runner"
 }
@@
   echo "doctor ok"
   echo "clerk issuer: $issuer"
   echo "seed user: $dev_email"
-  echo "api: http://localhost:8080"
-  echo "web: http://localhost:3000"
+  echo "api: $LOCAL_API_ORIGIN"
+  echo "web: $LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN"
+  if [[ "$PUBLIC_HOST" != "localhost" ]]; then
+    echo "tailscale api: $PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN"
+    echo "tailscale web: $PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN"
+  fi
 }
*** End Patch

payload.message:
Dev server is running over Tailscale:

```text
web: http://100.96.14.105:3000
api: http://100.96.14.105:8080
```

I verified in the browser via Tailscale:
- Loaded `/repos/adamblumoff/public-demo/settings`
- Navigated to `/repos/adamblumoff/public-demo`
- Loaded `/history`, clicked back to repo root
- Repo root rendered correctly, not blank
- Browser resources/API URLs no longer pointed at `localhost`

Committed the fix:

```text
eda97b1 Fix repo root navigation from child routes
```

What changed:
- Split repo root page into `web/src/routes/repos.$owner.$repo.index.tsx`
- Parent repo route now only owns shared live state and renders `<Outlet />`
- Removed the dead `kind: 'live'` / `return null` blank-page path
- Updated `dev/scope-dev` so remote dev works through Tailscale while preserving local API guardrails

Dev server is running over Tailscale:

```text
web: http://100.96.14.105:3000
api: http://100.96.14.105:8080
```

I verified in the browser via Tailscale:
- Loaded `/repos/adamblumoff/public-demo/settings`
- Navigated to `/repos/adamblumoff/public-demo`
- Loaded `/history`, clicked back to repo root
- Repo root rendered correctly, not blank

Committed the fix:

eda97b1 Fix repo root navigation from child routes

- Updated `dev/scope-dev` so remote dev works through Tailscale while preserving local API guardrails

settings.developer_instructions:
<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.


## T3 Code collaborative browser

You are running inside T3 Code. The `t3-code` MCP server is the product-native collaborative browser shared with the user. When it exposes `preview_*` tools, prefer those tools for browser navigation, inspection, interaction, screenshots, and recordings.

For browser work, first call `preview_status`. If no automation-capable preview is attached, call `preview_open` before concluding that the browser is unavailable. Then use `preview_navigate`, `preview_snapshot`, and the focused interaction tools. Prefer snapshot-provided locators over coordinates.

Do not switch to global browser skills, Chrome, Node REPL browser automation, standalone Playwright, or agent-browser merely because the preview is initially closed or a first call fails. Use an alternative browser system only when the T3 preview tools are absent, the user explicitly requests another browser, or `preview_open` returns an explicit unsupported/unavailable error. A failed T3 preview tool call should be inspected and retried with corrected arguments when the error is actionable.

</collaboration_mode>

@@ -1,103 +1,117 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 
 COMMAND="${1:-status}"
 case "$COMMAND" in
   doctor|up|down|status|reset) ;;
   *)
     echo "usage: $0 [doctor|up|down|status|reset]" >&2
     exit 2
     ;;
 esac
 
 ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
 STATE_DIR="$ROOT/.tmp/local-dev"
 DATA_DIR="$ROOT/.scope/dev"
 OBJECT_DIR="$DATA_DIR/objects"
 KEY_FILE="$DATA_DIR/object-key.txt"
 API_PID_FILE="$STATE_DIR/api.pid"
 WEB_PID_FILE="$STATE_DIR/web.pid"
 API_OUT_LOG="$STATE_DIR/api.out.log"
 API_ERR_LOG="$STATE_DIR/api.err.log"
 WEB_OUT_LOG="$STATE_DIR/web.out.log"
 WEB_ERR_LOG="$STATE_DIR/web.err.log"
+LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN="http://localhost:3000"
+LOCAL_API_ORIGIN="http://localhost:8080"
+PUBLIC_HOST="${SCOPE_DEV_PUBLIC_HOST:-}"
+
+if [[ -z "$PUBLIC_HOST" ]] && command -v tailscale >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  PUBLIC_HOST="$(tailscale ip -4 2>/dev/null | head -n 1 || true)"
+fi
+PUBLIC_HOST="${PUBLIC_HOST:-localhost}"
+PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN="http://$PUBLIC_HOST:3000"
+PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN="http://$PUBLIC_HOST:8080"
+CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE="$LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN"
+if [[ "$PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN" != "$LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN" ]]; then
+  CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE="$CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE,$PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN"
+fi
 
 if [[ -d "$HOME/.local/share/scope-vcs-tools/node-24/bin" ]]; then
   export PATH="$HOME/.local/share/scope-vcs-tools/node-24/bin:$PATH"
 fi
 if [[ -d "$HOME/.cargo/bin" ]]; then
   export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$PATH"
 fi
 
 ensure_dir() {
   mkdir -p "$1"
 }
 
 read_dotenv_value() {
   local path="$1"
   local key="$2"
   [[ -f "$path" ]] || return 0
 
   awk -v key="$key" '
     /^[[:space:]]*#/ { next }
     /^[[:space:]]*$/ { next }
     {
       split($0, parts, "=")
       name = parts[1]
       gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", name)
       if (name != key) {
         next
       }
       value = substr($0, index($0, "=") + 1)
       gsub(/^[[:space:]]+|[[:space:]]+$/, "", value)
       if ((substr(value, 1, 1) == "\"" && substr(value, length(value), 1) == "\"") ||
           (substr(value, 1, 1) == "'"'"'" && substr(value, length(value), 1) == "'"'"'")) {
         value = substr(value, 2, length(value) - 2)
       }
       print value
       exit
     }
   ' "$path"
 }
 
 get_clerk_issuer() {
   local publishable_key payload mod clerk_host
   publishable_key="$(read_dotenv_value "$ROOT/web/.env.local" "VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY")"
   if [[ -z "$publishable_key" ]]; then
     echo "web/.env.local must define VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
   if [[ "$publishable_key" != pk_test_* ]]; then
     echo "local dev requires a Clerk development publishable key (pk_test_)" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
 
   payload="${publishable_key#pk_test_}"
   payload="${payload//_/\/}"
   payload="${payload//-/+}"
   mod=$(( ${#payload} % 4 ))
   if [[ "$mod" -eq 2 ]]; then
     payload="${payload}=="
   elif [[ "$mod" -eq 3 ]]; then
     payload="${payload}="
   fi
 
   if ! clerk_host="$(printf '%s' "$payload" | base64 --decode 2>/dev/null)"; then
     echo "VITE_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY is not valid base64url" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
   clerk_host="${clerk_host%$}"
   if [[ "$clerk_host" != *.clerk.accounts.dev ]]; then
     echo "local dev Clerk issuer must end with .clerk.accounts.dev, got $clerk_host" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
   printf 'https://%s\n' "$clerk_host"
 }
 
 get_dev_user_email() {
   local email
   email="$(read_dotenv_value "$ROOT/.env.local" "SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL")"
   if [[ -z "$email" ]]; then
     echo "root .env.local must define SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL for seeded local repos" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
@@ -110,241 +124,245 @@ get_dev_user_handle() {
 
 get_object_key() {
   ensure_dir "$DATA_DIR"
   if [[ ! -f "$KEY_FILE" ]]; then
     openssl rand -base64 32 > "$KEY_FILE"
   fi
   tr -d '\r\n' < "$KEY_FILE"
 }
 
 assert_command() {
   if ! command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
     echo "$1 is required for local dev" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
 }
 
 port_owner() {
   local port="$1"
   ss -H -ltnp "sport = :$port" 2>/dev/null |
     sed -n 's/.*pid=\([0-9][0-9]*\).*/\1/p' |
     head -n 1
 }
 
 assert_port_free() {
   local port="$1"
   local name="$2"
   local owner
   owner="$(port_owner "$port")"
   if [[ -n "$owner" ]]; then
     echo "$name port $port is already in use by PID $owner" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
 }
 
 stop_pid_file() {
   local pid_file="$1"
   local pid
   [[ -f "$pid_file" ]] || return 0
   pid="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < "$pid_file")"
   if [[ "$pid" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
     kill -TERM -- "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
     for _ in {1..30}; do
       kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || break
       sleep 0.1
     done
     if kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
       kill -KILL -- "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
     fi
   fi
   rm -f "$pid_file"
 }
 
 stop_owned_processes() {
   stop_pid_file "$API_PID_FILE"
   stop_pid_file "$WEB_PID_FILE"
 }
 
 write_api_runner() {
   local issuer="$1"
   local dev_email="$2"
   local dev_handle="$3"
   local object_key="$4"
   local runner="$STATE_DIR/api.sh"
 
   cat > "$runner" <<EOF
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 cd "$ROOT/api"
 while IFS='=' read -r name _; do
   [[ "\$name" == RAILWAY_* ]] && unset "\$name"
   [[ "\$name" == SCOPE_BUCKET_* ]] && unset "\$name"
 done < <(env)
 unset DATABASE_URL
 export SCOPE_ENV="local"
 export SCOPE_METADATA_STORE="memory"
 export SCOPE_OBJECT_STORE="filesystem"
 export SCOPE_DATA_DIR="$DATA_DIR"
 export SCOPE_OBJECT_STORE_DIR="$OBJECT_DIR"
 export SCOPE_OBJECT_ENCRYPTION_KEY="$object_key"
 export PORT="8080"
-export SCOPE_APP_ORIGIN="http://localhost:3000"
-export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="http://localhost:8080"
+export SCOPE_APP_ORIGIN="$LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN"
+export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="$LOCAL_API_ORIGIN"
 export CLERK_ISSUER="$issuer"
 export CLERK_JWKS_URL="$issuer/.well-known/jwks.json"
-export CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES="http://localhost:3000"
+export CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES="$CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES_VALUE"
 export SCOPE_DEV_USER_EMAIL="$dev_email"
 EOF
   if [[ -n "$dev_handle" ]]; then
     printf 'export SCOPE_DEV_USER_HANDLE=%q\n' "$dev_handle" >> "$runner"
   else
     printf 'unset SCOPE_DEV_USER_HANDLE\n' >> "$runner"
   fi
   cat >> "$runner" <<'EOF'
 exec cargo run --features local-dev
 EOF
   chmod +x "$runner"
 }
 
 write_web_runner() {
   local runner="$STATE_DIR/web.sh"
 
   cat > "$runner" <<EOF
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 set -euo pipefail
 cd "$ROOT/web"
 while IFS='=' read -r name _; do
   [[ "\$name" == RAILWAY_* ]] && unset "\$name"
 done < <(env)
 export SCOPE_API_INTERNAL_URL="http://localhost:8080"
-export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="http://localhost:8080"
-exec pnpm dev
+export SCOPE_API_PUBLIC_URL="$PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN"
+exec pnpm dev --host 0.0.0.0
 EOF
   chmod +x "$runner"
 }
 
 start_api() {
   local issuer dev_email dev_handle object_key
   issuer="$(get_clerk_issuer)"
   dev_email="$(get_dev_user_email)"
   dev_handle="$(get_dev_user_handle)"
   object_key="$(get_object_key)"
   write_api_runner "$issuer" "$dev_email" "$dev_handle" "$object_key"
   setsid "$STATE_DIR/api.sh" > "$API_OUT_LOG" 2> "$API_ERR_LOG" &
   echo "$!" > "$API_PID_FILE"
 }
 
 start_web() {
   write_web_runner
   setsid "$STATE_DIR/web.sh" > "$WEB_OUT_LOG" 2> "$WEB_ERR_LOG" &
   echo "$!" > "$WEB_PID_FILE"
 }
 
 wait_http() {
   local url="$1"
   local seconds="$2"
   local deadline=$((SECONDS + seconds))
   while (( SECONDS < deadline )); do
     if curl -fsS --max-time 2 "$url" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
       return 0
     fi
     sleep 0.5
   done
   return 1
 }
 
 pid_status() {
   local pid_file="$1"
   local pid
   if [[ ! -f "$pid_file" ]]; then
     echo "stopped"
     return
   fi
   pid="$(tr -d '[:space:]' < "$pid_file")"
   if [[ "$pid" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
     echo "running pid $pid"
     return
   fi
   echo "stale"
 }
 
 doctor() {
   assert_command cargo
   assert_command curl
   assert_command git
   assert_command openssl
   assert_command pnpm
   assert_command setsid
   assert_command ss
 
   local issuer dev_email secret_key
   issuer="$(get_clerk_issuer)"
   dev_email="$(get_dev_user_email)"
   secret_key="$(read_dotenv_value "$ROOT/web/.env.local" "CLERK_SECRET_KEY")"
   if [[ -z "$secret_key" || "$secret_key" != sk_test_* ]]; then
     echo "web/.env.local must define a Clerk development secret key (sk_test_)" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
 
   echo "doctor ok"
   echo "clerk issuer: $issuer"
   echo "seed user: $dev_email"
-  echo "api: http://localhost:8080"
-  echo "web: http://localhost:3000"
+  echo "api: $LOCAL_API_ORIGIN"
+  echo "web: $LOCAL_WEB_ORIGIN"
+  if [[ "$PUBLIC_HOST" != "localhost" ]]; then
+    echo "tailscale api: $PUBLIC_API_ORIGIN"
+    echo "tailscale web: $PUBLIC_WEB_ORIGIN"
+  fi
 }
 
 up() {
   doctor >/dev/null
   stop_owned_processes
   assert_port_free 8080 "api"
   assert_port_free 3000 "web"
   ensure_dir "$STATE_DIR"
   ensure_dir "$DATA_DIR"
   rm -f "$API_OUT_LOG" "$API_ERR_LOG" "$WEB_OUT_LOG" "$WEB_ERR_LOG"
   start_api
   if ! wait_http "http://localhost:8080/readyz" 45; then
     echo "api did not become ready; see $API_ERR_LOG" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
   start_web
   if ! wait_http "http://localhost:3000" 45; then
     echo "web did not become ready; see $WEB_ERR_LOG" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
   status
 }
 
 status() {
   echo "api process: $(pid_status "$API_PID_FILE")"
   echo "web process: $(pid_status "$WEB_PID_FILE")"
   if body="$(curl -fsS --max-time 3 "http://localhost:8080/readyz" 2>/dev/null)"; then
     echo "api readyz: $body"
   else
     echo "api readyz: unavailable"
   fi
   if code="$(curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' --max-time 3 "http://localhost:3000" 2>/dev/null)"; then
     echo "web: HTTP $code"
   else
     echo "web: unavailable"
   fi
 }
 
 reset() {
   stop_owned_processes
   local resolved_data resolved_root
   resolved_data="$(realpath -m "$DATA_DIR")"
   resolved_root="$(realpath -m "$ROOT")"
   if [[ "$resolved_data" != "$resolved_root"/* ]]; then
     echo "refusing to remove data outside repo: $resolved_data" >&2
     exit 1
   fi
   rm -rf "$DATA_DIR" "$STATE_DIR"
   echo "local dev state reset"
 }
 
 case "$COMMAND" in
   doctor) doctor ;;
   up) up ;;
   down) stop_owned_processes; echo "local dev stopped" ;;
   status) status ;;
   reset) reset ;;
 esac

index 0000000..70815c1

@@ -0,0 +1,59 @@
+import {
+  loadRepoForRequest,
+  parseRepoParams,
+  parseSetRepoFileVisibilityInput,
+  setRepoFileVisibilityForRequest,
+} from '@/api/repos'
+import type { RepoParams, ReviewFile, Visibility } from '@/api/types'
+import { RepoDetailPage } from '@/features/repo-detail/repo-detail-page'
+import { createFileRoute, redirect } from '@tanstack/react-router'
+import { createServerFn } from '@tanstack/react-start'
+
+const loadRepo = createServerFn({ method: 'GET' })
+  .validator(parseRepoParams)
+  .handler(({ data }) => loadRepoForRequest(data))
+
+const setRepoFileVisibility = createServerFn({ method: 'POST' })
+  .validator(parseSetRepoFileVisibilityInput)
+  .handler(({ data }) => setRepoFileVisibilityForRequest(data))
+
+export const Route = createFileRoute('/repos/$owner/$repo/')({
+  loader: async ({ params }) => {
+    const detail = await loadRepo({ data: params })
+    if (detail.review) {
+      throw redirect({
+        params,
+        to: '/repos/$owner/$repo/review',
+      })
+    }
+    return detail
+  },
+  component: RepoIndexRoute,
+})
+
+function RepoIndexRoute() {
+  const detail = Route.useLoaderData()
+  const params = Route.useParams()
+
+  return (
+    <RepoDetailPage
+      detail={detail}
+      setFileVisibility={setLiveRepoFileVisibility}
+      params={params}
+    />
+  )
+}
+
+function setLiveRepoFileVisibility(
+  params: RepoParams,
+  files: ReviewFile[],
+  visibility: Visibility,
+) {
+  return setRepoFileVisibility({
+    data: {
+      ...params,
+      paths: files.map((file) => file.path),
+      visibility,
+    },
+  })
+}

# AGENTS.md instructions for /home/adam-blumoff/code/scope-vcs

<INSTRUCTIONS>
# Global Agent Preferences

These are durable preferences distilled from local agent history. Project instructions and the current user request always take precedence.

## Working Style

- Prefer action once direction is clear. When the user says to go ahead, implement and verify instead of stopping at another proposal.
- For complex, destructive, architectural, or ambiguous work, first explain the root cause or tradeoff, give a concrete plan, and ask targeted questions if the answer changes the approach.
- Do not assume. Inspect the repo, running app, logs, screenshots, or device state directly when the user asks what is happening.
- Keep the user updated with concrete findings, not generic status. Use simple language when explaining failures or architecture.

## Engineering Taste

- Keep implementations as simple as the scope allows. Less is better than more unless there is a strong reason to add machinery.
- Favor long-term maintainable fixes over local patches. Remove dead code, collapse duplicated logic, and simplify tangled flows while fixing bugs.
- When a system feels strung together, consider subtracting or redesigning the flow instead of layering another workaround on top.
- Tests should be useful regression protection, not coverage theater. Prefer the test level that catches real failures quickly, including E2E for important UI, IPC, connection, or workflow behavior.

## Frontend And Product Work

- Check UI work in the running app yourself. Use browser/Playwright/screenshots or available visual tools to verify layout, alignment, interaction, and loading states.
- Strongly prefer polished, minimal, fast interfaces. Avoid boxy/card-heavy layouts when a clean text, list, or section treatment will work.
- Alignment and spacing matter. Fix visible UI details before calling work done, especially responsive wrapping, centered/left-aligned elements, hover states, and clipped content.
- For redesigns, be willing to be bold and creative, but keep the product usable and wired to existing behavior unless the user explicitly allows backend changes.
- Use named frontend/design/performance skills when the user calls them out, and apply them before implementing.

## Git And Delivery

- Commit, push, merge, or create branches only when the user asks. When asked to commit, verify first and report anything left uncommitted.
- Use a new branch for risky, destructive, or experimental work when requested. Keep focused fixes on the smallest reasonable change surface.
- If CI matters for the request, keep going until it is green or explain the blocker with the exact failing check.
- For firmware, hardware, or device workflows, upload/run/monitor directly when possible and report what happened, including verification gaps.

## Output Expectations

- Summaries should lead with what changed, what was verified, and what remains risky.
- For bug investigations, include the root cause and why the previous behavior failed before describing the fix.
- For plans, make them concrete enough to execute: files or areas to inspect, implementation steps, validation steps, and rollback or migration notes when relevant.

--- project-doc ---

This product is still in pre-alpha and we have zero users and zero valuable data. Every change should be destructive, no legacy or backwards compatible bullshit, this application has no users or releases, treat it as such. If you add backwards compatibility, I will go apeshit on your dumbass.

We want the architecture centered around durable domain code: the layer that defines the core concepts, rules, allowed transitions, invariants, and required side effects independent of any delivery mechanism. Outer layers should stay thin and predictable: translate inputs and outputs, call domain behavior, persist or render results, and surface errors without inventing their own rules.

Keep sources of truth singular, make side effects explicit, and prefer small behavior-owned modules over broad catch-all files. When ownership gets blurry, refactor toward clearer boundaries; when code exists only for speculation, compatibility, or half-owned future surfaces, delete it.

Testing code should be seperate from source code, only combine if you have a good justification to do so.

Please don't use cards for ui, only use them if absolutely necessary.

No file should be over 1000 lines of code, at that point do an audit of the file and modularize.

In general, I trust you with refactors as they don't effect the behavior of the application. However, on behavior making changes I want to be very involved and make sure we go slow and methodically. 

Autoreview timeout should be set to 15 minutes

</INSTRUCTIONS>
